data.narrative:
Xanadu Quantum Technologies is a public-stage quantum computing company that has raised $245M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 6 public-stage quantum computing companies, spanning IonQ ($11.0B), Quantinuum ($10.0B), Rigetti Computing ($5.5B), D-Wave Quantum ($2.0B), and Infleqtion ($1.8B), with a range floor at $60M.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields an implied valuation of $3.8B. This represents the median across the six-company cohort and reflects market pricing for public quantum computing operators at similar developmental stages. The peer range extends from $60M to $11.0B, indicating material dispersion in how the market values quantum technology approaches and commercial readiness.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $3.2B (low) to $4.3B (high), centered at $3.8B. This narrow band reflects the stability of the peer-median estimate but is constrained by reliance on a single methodological input. Key uncertainties include differentiation in quantum architecture (photonic versus ion trap versus superconducting approaches), path to revenue generation, and whether near-term technical milestones will compress or widen the current peer valuation dispersion. Additional triangulation via precedent transaction or discounted cash flow analysis would strengthen conviction in this range.
